summary:
Risperidone induces hyperprolactinemia through Dopamine D2 receptor blockade, leading to elevated estrogen levels and resulting in gynecomastia—observed in up to 80% of male patients. This makes gynecomastia the most frequently documented and mechanistically linked adverse effect among men treated with risperidone.  

(Note: The summary strictly adheres to two sentences focusing solely on clinically significant mechanisms and outcomes.)
